assertions.description: The PCB correctly reports a 2-layer board (F.Cu, B.Cu), 25.4x25.4mm dimensions, 69 total footprints (28 SMD + 1 THT + 35 board-only + 4 solder mask bridge + 1 exclude-from-pos), 153 track segments, 94 vias, 5 zones, 21 nets, and routing_complete=true. The high via count (94) reflects heavy GND stitching on the small board.

The thermal analysis correctly identifies a GND zone spanning both F.Cu and B.Cu (645.2mm² area) with 77 stitching vias at 11.9/cm² density. Separate 3.3V (41.7mm²) and 5V (33.9mm²) zones on F.Cu are also correctly identified. The zone analysis reflects the compact 25.4×25.4mm board with heavy power plane coverage.

DFM reports dfm_tier='standard', min_track_width=0.254mm, min_drill=0.3mm, min_annular_ring=0.13mm, and zero violations. These metrics are appropriate for a standard Qwiic breakout board manufactured at typical PCB fab tolerances.

INCORRECT: The KiCad schematic file is shared across multiple projects. The schematic analyzer reads the 'co2' project instance references (U1 for AP3012) while the PCB uses references from a different project instance (U5). Both correctly point to the same physical AP3012 IC, but cross-referencing schematic vs PCB analysis outputs is misleading. The PCB component_groups 'U' group lists only U5.
